entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal grain crop used in rotation with groundnut.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop whose yield is affected by wheat straw incorporation.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of crop production, specifically grain yield for wheat and overall yield for groundnut.

entities.name: N UPTAKE
entities.description: The absorption of nitrogen by groundnut plants.

entities.name: INCORPORATION OF WHEAT STRAW
entities.description: The process of adding undecomposed wheat straw into the soil.

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of adding specific microbial decomposers to wheat straw.

entities.name: PENICILLIUM DIGITATUM
entities.description: A microbial decomposer used to inoculate wheat straw.

entities.name: STAPHYLOTRICHUM COCCOSPORUM
entities.description: A microbial decomposer used to inoculate wheat straw.

entities.name: MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: General microbial agents used for the decomposition of wheat straw.

entities.name: C:P RATIO
entities.description: The carbon to phosphorus ratio of the wheat straw.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC C AND N CONTENT
entities.description: The levels of organic carbon and nitrogen present in the soil.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: The process of breaking down organic matter using microorganisms.

relationships.description: Increases the yield of groundnut and subsequent wheat crops
Increases the yield of groundnut when combined with straw incorporation
Increased groundnut yield by 37% when C:P ratio was 65
Narrower ratios generally gave higher groundnut yields while a 200 ratio gave highest wheat yield
Increases nitrogen uptake in groundnut plants
Increases nitrogen uptake in groundnut plants

relationships.target: SOIL ORGANIC C AND N CONTENT
relationships.description: Increases the organic carbon and nitrogen content of the soil

relationships.target: SOIL ORGANIC C AND N CONTENT
relationships.description: Produced the highest increase in soil organic C and N at a 65 C:P ratio
The objective of incorporating straw and microorganisms is to facilitate biological decomposition
Microorganisms are the agents that carry out biological decomposition
